title: Realtime Message Envelope
description: A canonical envelope describing a single message flowing across a realtime channel — the framing that providers wrap around user payloads. This envelope generalizes the message shapes used by Ably, Pusher, PubNub, Socket.IO, MQTT 5 (with user properties), graphql-ws, and CloudEvents-over-WebSocket.

id.description: A unique identifier for the message — typically a ULID, UUID, or provider-assigned message ID. Used for deduplication and resume-from-checkpoint.

channel.description: Channel name to which the message was published.

event.description: Application-defined event name or message type (e.g., 'message.created', 'order.updated', 'user.joined'). Maps to Ably 'name', Pusher 'event', Socket.IO event names, and CloudEvents 'type'.

data.description: The application payload. Often JSON, but may be a base64-encoded binary buffer, plain text, or a primitive value.

encoding.description: Encoding of the data field when not JSON.

contentType.description: IANA media type of the payload (e.g., 'application/json', 'application/vnd.example.event+json').

publishedAt.description: Time the message was accepted by the realtime provider.

receivedAt.description: Time the message was delivered to the subscribing client (set client-side).

clientId.description: Identifier of the publishing client connection or user. Maps to Ably clientId, MQTT ClientID, Socket.IO socket.id.

connectionId.description: Provider-assigned identifier for the transport connection that originated the message.

qualityOfService.description: Delivery guarantee applied to this message.

sequence.description: Per-channel monotonically increasing sequence number for ordering and gap detection.

correlationId.description: Correlation identifier linking this message to a request, trace, or higher-level workflow.

replyTo.description: Channel name on which the publisher expects a response (request/response pattern over pub/sub).

expiresAt.description: Time at which the message is considered stale and may be discarded by the broker or consumer.

headers.description: Provider-level metadata or user properties (modeled on MQTT 5 User Properties, AMQP application properties, and HTTP headers).

trace.description: Distributed tracing context propagated with the message.
